articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

Goal! FC Bayern München 4, Eintracht Frankfurt 0. Serge Gnabry (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Kingsley Coman.

video.headline: Bayern keep their title charge on track with thrashing of Frankfurt
video.caption: Bayern Munich ease past Eintracht Frankfurt 4-0 in the Bundesliga.
video.title: Bayern keep their title charge on track with thrashing of Frankfurt
video.description: Bayern Munich ease past Eintracht Frankfurt 4-0 in the Bundesliga.

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Highlight (Bayern keep their title charge on track with thrashing of Frankfurt) 2025/02/23 GLOBAL US ONLY

article.story:
Bundesliga leaders Bayern Munich powered past third-placed Eintracht Frankfurt 4-0 with an impressive display of high pressing football on Sunday to restore their eight-point lead in the Bundesliga over second-placed Bayer Leverkusen.

The Bavarians were dominant throughout, while successfully nipping any planned Eintracht attack in the bud, and earning their first win in three matches across all competitions following two consecutive draws.

They are on 58 points in top spot with champions Leverkusen, 2-0 winners at Holstein Kiel on Saturday, in second place with 50. Eintracht are third on 42.

"Now we have won but we will stay calm as we did the last few days [after draws against Leverkusen last week and Celtic in the Champions League on Tuesday]," Bayern coach Vincent Kompany told a press conference. "The couple of days leading into the game we felt the team was sharper. There were a lot of reasons for us to get a result today.

"The intensity from both sides was pretty high from the start and we managed to tilt the game in our favour. The players showed they were hungry today."

Bayern shot out of the blocks in a pulsating first half and had several golden chances, with Eintracht boxed into their own half, before Michael Olise tapped in a Leroy Sané cutback in stoppage time to give them a fully-deserved lead.

Bayern, who will face Leverkusen in the Champions League Round of 16 next month, suffered a blow with Joshua Kimmich taken off injured just before the break, but rebounded after.

Kompany said Kimmich's injury did not look too serious.

They kept up the relentless pressure, adding a second goal with Hiroki Ito in the 61st after Eintracht keeper Kevin Trapp misjudged a corner.

It was the Japan international's first goal for his team and he became the first player from his country to score a league goal for Bayern.

With Harry Kane coming on as a second-half substitute following his recovery from a calf injury, the hosts should have scored a third in the 78th but Sane, in front of goal, fired wide.

Jamal Musiala did it better in the 83rd, charging forward and finishing the move with a solo effort. With the Eintracht backline in tatters, substitute Serge Gnabry slotted in their fourth in stoppage time.
